reforms.description: Hungary implemented several supported programmes aimed at enhancing research and technological development, including competitions for applied research and the improvement of research infrastructure.
reforms.package_name: Hungarian Research and Development Support Programmes
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These programmes are designed to stimulate innovation and improve the competitiveness of Hungarian firms through enhanced research capabilities.
reforms.status_evidence: The supported programmes have been adopted and are currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: These programmes represent a significant effort to bolster R&D activities in Hungary, contributing to the overall innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Supported programmes include: Applied Research Competition, Competition for the Improvement of RTD Infrastructural Conditions, Competition for the Development of New Exportable Products.

reforms.description: In 1997, Hungary revised employer contributions to the health-care system to combat under-reporting of salaries, introducing a minimum contribution while adjusting rates for higher-paid workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to increase compliance and broaden the tax base, potentially leading to more stable funding for health care and improved public health outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The changes to employer contributions took effect on 1 January 1997.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to the health-care funding mechanism, addressing issues of tax evasion and compliance, which are critical for the sustainability of public health financing.
reforms.source_quote: As of 1 January 1997 employer contributions to the health-care system were revised.

reforms.description: A new active labour market programme was set up in 1997 aimed at the long-term unemployed who have never benefited from an ALMP, providing interviews, career counselling, and subsidised employment where necessary.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the employability of long-term unemployed individuals, thereby increasing the overall skill level of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was set up to provide necessary support for the long-term unemployed.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to address long-term unemployment and improve the skills of a vulnerable population segment.
reforms.source_quote: A new ALMP programme was set up in 1997 aimed at the long-term unemployed who have never benefited from an ALMP.

reforms.description: Support for persons starting an enterprise was provided through a new Start programme, which covers startup-related costs and offers training for unemployed workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages entrepreneurship and self-employment, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Start programme was initiated to support unemployed individuals in starting their own businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme provides essential support for entrepreneurship, which is crucial for economic dynamism and job creation.
reforms.source_quote: Support for persons starting an enterprise was initially provided by the Re-start programme which was replaced by a Start programme.

reforms.description: The Hungarian government introduced tax incentives for R&D expenditures, allowing firms to deduct 100% of their R&D costs from taxable income, and introduced personal income tax exemptions for income earned from intellectual property.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These tax incentives are designed to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Firms can reduce their pre-tax profits by 20% of their direct R&D and experimental expenditures as of January 1, 1997.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the fiscal policy towards encouraging R&D investment, which is crucial for enhancing Hungary's innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: As of 1 January 1997 firms can, for tax purposes, reduce their pre-tax profits by 20 per cent of their direct R&D and experimental expenditures.

reforms.description: The Central Technological Development Fund was established to promote technological transfer and regional development, particularly among small and medium enterprises, through various distribution mechanisms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating technology transfer and supporting SMEs, the fund aims to enhance innovation and economic development in the regions.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund emphasizes technological transfer, diffusion and regional development, especially among small and medium enterprises.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for enhancing regional development and supporting SMEs, which are vital for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Central Technological Development Fund emphasises technological transfer, diffusion and regional development, especially among small and medium enterprises.
